The Pour Decision

Built a hospitality and beverage channel from zero

The Pour Decision was a short-form content channel built around Melbourne's hospitality and beverage scene. The concept was simple: identify an underserved audience, build repeatable content formats around what they actually wanted to watch, and grow it consistently. We uploaded consistently for two and a half years, treating social media as a distribution channel for the businesses and brands we worked with.

Every piece of content started with research. I used TikTok Creative Center and Instagram insights to identify trending formats, sounds and hooks before they peaked, then applied that to each shoot. After posting I pulled the retention graphs, found the drop-off points and refined the edit, pacing and captions next time. The process got tighter with every video.

The channel grew to 74,000 followers and 10 million views. We secured national brand partnerships including Hard Rated, Ninja, Australian Venue Co and Grays Online, writing scripts and producing content that drove in-venue foot traffic and commercial outcomes for each partner. Multiple venue owners reported direct increases in foot traffic tied to our content.

Departed Spirits
Brick Lane Market Launch
On-site event content

Managed Instagram end-to-end for Departed Spirits including content calendars, scripting, on-site filming at events and product launches, editing and publishing. Took a relatively inactive account to hundreds of thousands of monthly views. Built repeatable organic content designed to educate viewers on the product and drive purchase intent. The best performing organic content was then used to inform a paid Meta strategy, pushing spend behind proven hooks and formats. This combined approach contributed to a 446% lift in online store revenue, 148% increase in website sessions and an 82% improvement in conversion rate.

Instagram insights — 304,978 views, 205% uplift over previous 28 days

Sales Experience

Sales & Commercial Experience

Senior Accounts Manager at Wine Concierge Service, generating over $100,000 in over the phone sales. Managed a portfolio of high value clients, consistently exceeded monthly KPIs and thrived in a fast-paced, high energy team environment.

About

A bit about me

I studied economics and finance at RMIT while working in sales. That background shapes how I think about marketing. I understand that views and audience are distribution, and that distribution is what drives commercial outcomes. I am drawn to the numbers as much as the creative side. Retention graphs, conversion rates, watch curves. That combination is what I think makes the difference between marketing that looks good and marketing that actually does something.

I started The Pour Decision because I saw an underserved audience and understood how to reach them. I have a strong sales background, I love working in a team, I understand the importance of trust with clients and brands, and I thrive in fast-paced environments where there is a clear commercial goal. I am looking to bring everything I have built into a full-time role where content, data and sales thinking all work together.

Based South Yarra, Melbourne
Education B. Business (Economics & Finance), RMIT
Editing CapCut, Canva
Platforms TikTok, Instagram, Meta Ads
Strengths Hook writing, trend monitoring, on-camera
